title: Accelerating Rotarod: Motor Coordination and Balance Assessment in Mice
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"d6qul","text":"This protocol describes the assessment of motor coordination and balance using an accelerating rotarod in adult mice. After a training phase on the rotating rod at constant speed, mice undergo four test trials on an accelerating rod, and latency to fall is recorded.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"8lptb","text":"•       Ensure consistent placement on the rod — mouse should be placed mid-rod with all four paws gripping.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8cviq","text":"•       Clean rod with 70% ethanol between animals to remove olfactory cues.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"as1p9","text":"•       All testing should be performed at the same time of day.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"95i3l","text":"•       Exclude animals that jump off within the first 5 s of a trial; repeat that trial.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"87ioa","text":"•       Calibrate rotarod: confirm acceleration rate (5→40 rpm over 5 min) and constant-speed setting (5 rpm).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"an69e","text":"•       Allow mice to acclimate to the procedure room for ≥30 min before testing.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"88spu","text":"•       Confirm the automatic timer stops at 300 s maximum.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"73roj","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"55mos","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"5dttn","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"tables","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"cellsMeta":{"0_0":"","0_1":"","1_0":"","1_1":"","2_0":"","2_1":"","3_0":"","3_1":"","4_0":"","4_1":"","5_0":"","5_1":"","6_0":"","6_1":""},"colTitles":["",""],"colWidths":[140,"371"],"data":[["\u003cb\u003eSubjects\u003c/b\u003e","Adult WT and KO littermates (~2 months old)"],["\u003cb\u003eApparatus\u003c/b\u003e","Rotarod (Ugo Basile, Italy)"],["\u003cb\u003eTraining speed\u003c/b\u003e","5 rpm (constant) × 2 min on rotating rod"],["\u003cb\u003eTest protocol\u003c/b\u003e","Accelerating: 5 → 40 rpm over 5 min (300 s)"],["\u003cb\u003eNumber of trials\u003c/b\u003e","4 test trials per animal"],["\u003cb\u003eInter-trial interval\u003c/b\u003e","10 min"],["\u003cb\u003eEndpoint\u003c/b\u003e","Latency to fall (max 300 s); mean of 4 trials"]],"guid":"34F54BA6F6AA48B9B0C14C9021553E94","isJexcelDataFormat":true,"legend":"","mergeCells":{},"printData":[["\u003cb\u003eSubjects\u003c/b\u003e","Adult WT and KO littermates (~2 months old)"],["\u003cb\u003eApparatus\u003c/b\u003e","Rotarod (Ugo Basile, Italy)"],["\u003cb\u003eTraining speed\u003c/b\u003e","5 rpm (constant) × 2 min on rotating rod"],["\u003cb\u003eTest protocol\u003c/b\u003e","Accelerating: 5 → 40 rpm over 5 min (300 s)"],["\u003cb\u003eNumber of trials\u003c/b\u003e","4 test trials per animal"],["\u003cb\u003eInter-trial interval\u003c/b\u003e","10 min"],["\u003cb\u003eEndpoint\u003c/b\u003e","Latency to fall (max 300 s); mean of 4 trials"]],"rowHeights":[23,23,23,23,23,23,23]}}}}
steps: Training phase Place each mouse on the rotating rod already set to constant speed (5 rpm). Allow the mouse to walk on the rod for 2 min. If the mouse drops during training, place it back on the rod (up to 3 times). This familiarizes the animal with the apparatus. Return mouse to home cage after training.

Test trials - setup Place all mice onto the rotating rod at 5 rpm, one at a time, ensuring all four paws are gripping mid-rod. Once all mice are positioned, start the acceleration protocol. The rod accelerates from 5 rpm to 40 rpm over 5 min (300 s). Record latency to drop (in seconds) for each mouse. Maximum trial duration is 300 s. If a mouse clings to the rod and does not walk on it, observe for a maximum of 3 full rotations to allow it to resume walking. If the mouse continues clinging after 3 rotations, remove it from the apparatus and record the time at that point as the latency.
Inter-trial rest (10 min) Return each mouse to its home cage for a 10-min inter-trial interval. Repeat Steps 2 and 3 for trials 2, 3, and 4.
Complete all 4 trials Each mouse completes 4 test trials with 10-min inter-trial intervals. Record latency to fall for each trial. Calculate the mean latency across all 4 trials per animal.
Data analysis Express performance as mean latency to fall ± SEM across the 4 test trials per animal. Compare WT vs KO using appropriate statistics.
creator: Sarah van Veen
